story.iWant: to manage my linked login methods from my account
story.soThat: I can add new providers, remove ones I no longer use, and set a password as a backup
story.tasks: - [ ] Add repository methods to `repository/user.go` (AC: #1-#8) - [ ] `GetUserAuthProviders(userID)` — return all auth rows for user (AC: #1) - [ ] `LinkProvider(userID, provider, email, avatarURL)` — insert new auth row (AC: #2) - [ ] `GetAuthByProviderEmail(provider, email)` — check if provider email belongs to another user (AC: #3) - [ ] `UnlinkProvider(userID, provider)` — delete auth row (AC: #4) - [ ] `CountAuthMethods(userID)` — count auth rows for lockout check (AC: #5) - [ ] `HasPassword(userID)` — check if "local" auth row with password exists (AC: #6, #8) - [ ] `SetPassword(userID, hashedPassword)` — create "local" auth row (AC: #6) - [ ] `UpdatePassword(userID, hashedPassword)` — update existing password hash (AC: #7) - [ ] `GetPasswordHash(userID)` — get hash for verification (AC: #7) - [ ] Create `handlers/account.go` with request/response structs (AC: #1-#8) - [ ] `GetLinkedProviders` — list all providers (AC: #1) - [ ] `LinkProvider` — validate + insert, check for cross-user conflict (AC: #2, #3) - [ ] `UnlinkProvider` — validate lockout protection + delete (AC: #4, #5) - [ ] `SetPassword` — validate no existing password + hash + create (AC: #6, #8) - [ ] `ChangePassword` — verify current + hash new + update (AC: #7) - [ ] Create `routes/account.go` — authenticated route group (AC: #1-#8) - [ ] `GET /api/account/providers` → `GetLinkedProviders` - [ ] `POST /api/account/link-provider` → `LinkProvider` - [ ] `DELETE /api/account/providers/:provider` → `UnlinkProvider` - [ ] `POST /api/account/set-password` → `SetPassword` - [ ] `PUT /api/account/change-password` → `ChangePassword` - [ ] Register account routes in main router setup - [ ] Create `handlers/account_test.go` with table-driven tests (AC: #9) - [ ] Test GetLinkedProviders: returns correct provider list - [ ] Test LinkProvider: happy path, duplicate provider, cross-user conflict - [ ] Test UnlinkProvider: happy path, lockout protection (last method) - [ ] Test SetPassword: happy path, password already exists error - [ ] Test ChangePassword: happy path, wrong current password - [ ] Test all endpoints: unauthenticated request returns 401 - [ ] Add repository tests for new methods in `repository/user_test.go` (AC: #9)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC #1: Given an authenticated user, when they call `GET /api/account/providers`, then they receive a list of their linked providers with provider name, avatar URL, and linked date.
AC #2: Given an authenticated user, when they call `POST /api/account/link-provider` with valid OAuth data, then a new auth row is created and the updated provider list is returned.
AC #3: Given User A has GitHub linked, when User B tries to link the same GitHub account (same provider email), then User B receives a 409 error "This GitHub account is already linked to another account."
AC #4: Given a user with 2+ auth methods, when they call `DELETE /api/account/providers/:provider`, then the auth row is removed and the updated list is returned.
AC #5: Given a user with only 1 auth method remaining, when they try to unlink it, then they receive a 400 error "Cannot remove your only login method."
AC #6: Given an OAuth-only user (no password), when they call `POST /api/account/set-password` with a valid password, then a "local" auth row is created with the bcrypt-hashed password.
AC #7: Given a user with a password set, when they call `PUT /api/account/change-password` with correct current password and new password, then the password hash is updated.
AC #8: Given a user with a password set, when they call `POST /api/account/set-password`, then they receive a 400 error "Password already set. Use change-password instead."
AC #9: All new endpoints have comprehensive table-driven tests covering happy path, validation errors, auth errors, and edge cases.

doc.title: Multi-Provider Auth & Rate Limit Tuning Tech Spec
doc.section: Implementation Details — Phase 2: Account Management Endpoints
doc.snippet: Contains link/unlink/set-password flow pseudocode, lockout protection logic, request/response contracts, error code patterns, and existing conventions to follow.

doc.title: Ditto Backend Architecture
doc.section: Architecture Pattern, API Design, Authentication and Security
doc.snippet: Defines the Handler → Repository → Database layered architecture, response format (ApiResponse), JWT auth flow, and error handling patterns used throughout the backend.

doc.title: Epic 8: Multi-Provider Authentication & Account Security
doc.snippet: Epic-level acceptance criteria and story map showing Story 8.2 depends on Story 8.1 (multi-provider schema). Defines link/unlink providers, set/change password, and lockout protection goals.

doc.title: Story 8.2: Account Management Endpoints
doc.section: Implementation Details, Key Code References, Context References
doc.snippet: Full story with tasks/subtasks, technical summary, project structure notes, and key code reference table mapping existing code locations to reuse patterns.

file.reason: Reference pattern for new AccountHandler struct, request/response structs, OAuthRequest struct (reuse for LinkProviderRequest), password verification with bcrypt (Login handler), and OAuth response format (OAuthLogin handler).

Primary file to extend with new methods: GetUserAuthProviders, LinkProvider, GetAuthByProviderEmail, UnlinkProvider, CountAuthMethods, HasPassword, SetPassword, UpdatePassword, GetPasswordHash. Already contains multi-provider support from Story 8.1.
UserAuth struct used for auth rows — may need a UserAuthProvider response struct for the providers listing endpoint that includes provider name, avatar URL, and linked date.
Route registration pattern to follow for new RegisterAccountRoutes function — uses middleware grouping (Auth + CSRF) for protected endpoints.
User-based rate limiting middleware (RateLimiter.Middleware) can be applied to account endpoints. IP-based limiters already configured from Story 8.1.
Valid provider constants for validation in link/unlink operations. IsValidOAuthProvider for input validation.
Bcrypt password hashing/verification functions to use in SetPassword and ChangePassword handlers.
Error handling helpers used by all handlers — AccountHandler should use these same functions.
Route registration location where RegisterAccountRoutes must be added to the apiGroup.
AppState struct passed to handler constructors — AccountHandler will follow same NewAccountHandler(appState) pattern.
Test database setup used by all handler and repository tests. Schema already includes multi-provider users_auth (UNIQUE user_id, auth_provider) and user_refresh_tokens from Story 8.1.
Test environment setup pattern to replicate for account handler tests — creates test DB, app state, handler, and router with routes.
Repository test pattern — table-driven tests with testutil.NewTestDatabase, RunMigrations, and testify assertions.
Error codes to use: ErrorConflict (409), ErrorBadRequest (400), ErrorUnauthorized (401), ErrorNotFound (404). New handlers should return these standard error codes.
Response helper functions — all handlers use response.Success(c, data) and response.Error(c, appErr) for consistent JSON responses.

dependencies.note: No new dependencies required — all work uses existing packages.

interface.name: GET /api/account/providers
interface.signature: GET /api/account/providers → { success: true, data: [{ auth_provider, avatar_url, created_at }] }

interface.name: POST /api/account/link-provider
interface.signature: POST /api/account/link-provider { provider, email, name, avatar_url } → { success: true, data: [providers] } | 409 conflict

interface.name: DELETE /api/account/providers/:provider
interface.signature: DELETE /api/account/providers/:provider → { success: true, data: [providers] } | 400 last method

interface.name: POST /api/account/set-password
interface.signature: POST /api/account/set-password { password } → { success: true, data: { message } } | 400 already set

interface.name: PUT /api/account/change-password
interface.signature: PUT /api/account/change-password { current_password, new_password } → { success: true, data: { message } } | 401 wrong password

interface.name: UserRepository (extended)
interface.signature: GetUserAuthProviders(userID) ([]UserAuth, error), LinkProvider(userID, provider, email, avatarURL) error, GetAuthByProviderEmail(provider, email) (*UserAuth, error), UnlinkProvider(userID, provider) error, CountAuthMethods(userID) (int, error), HasPassword(userID) (bool, error), SetPassword(userID, hash) error, UpdatePassword(userID, hash) error, GetPasswordHash(userID) (string, error)

constraints.constraint: Follow existing Handler → Repository → Database layered architecture pattern
All request structs must use `json` and `validate` tags; validate with `validator.Struct()`
Use standard response format: `{ success: true, data: {...} }` or `{ success: false, error: { error: "msg", code: "CODE" } }`
All endpoints require JWT authentication (AuthMiddleware) and CSRF protection (CSRFMiddleware)
Repository methods use raw SQL via sqlx — no ORM
Password hashing uses bcrypt via `auth.HashPassword()` and `auth.CheckPassword()`
Lockout protection: cannot remove last auth method (CountAuthMethods must be > 1 before unlink)
Tests must be table-driven using testify, with testutil.NewTestDatabase for DB setup
Error handling must use HandleError/HandleErrorWithMessage from helpers.go
Only add comments that explain WHY, not WHAT — code should be self-documenting

tests.standards: Backend tests use Go's testing package with testify v1.10.0 for assertions (require for fatal, assert for non-fatal). Table-driven test pattern with descriptive test names. Test database setup via testutil.NewTestDatabase + RunMigrations. Handler tests create a gin test router and use httptest.NewRecorder. Repository tests operate directly against the test database. Run with `go test -p 1 ./...` for sequential execution (shared test DB).

ideas.idea: Test GetLinkedProviders returns correct provider list for user with single and multiple providers
Test LinkProvider happy path: authenticated user adds new provider, verify response contains updated list
Test LinkProvider cross-user conflict: User B links provider email already owned by User A → 409
Test LinkProvider duplicate: user tries to link already-linked provider → appropriate error
Test UnlinkProvider happy path: user with 2+ methods removes one, verify response
Test UnlinkProvider lockout: user with only 1 method tries to unlink → 400
Test SetPassword happy path: OAuth-only user sets password, verify local auth row created
Test ChangePassword happy path: correct current password, new password updated
Test ChangePassword wrong current password → 401/400 error
Test SetPassword when password exists → 400 "Password already set"
Test all endpoints return 401 when unauthenticated
Test all endpoints with invalid/missing request body → validation errors
Repository unit tests for each new method: GetUserAuthProviders, LinkProvider, GetAuthByProviderEmail, UnlinkProvider, CountAuthMethods, HasPassword, SetPassword, UpdatePassword, GetPasswordHash
